video.title: RIP the Supertuck | Volta a Catalunya Stage 5 2021

transcript.clean_text: welcome back to the lantern rouge youtube channel into highlights of stage five the vultura catalunya 2021 esteban chavez basking in the glory of his magnificent stage win yesterday 193 kilometer stage from the public segue to manrasa a rolling course with over 2 500 meters climbing with not too much to offer the gc men with the climbs only being eight k's at 5.2 percent and the last climb 7.3 k's at 6.7 cresting over 20k from the finish but it would prove to be a big headache for ineos with adam yates in the leader's jersey now we didn't have live images for the early part of the stage where a lot of the chaos unfolded in this stage five and that chaos was related to the early break formation now i'm just going off the live feed on the vulture catalonia website to fill in some of these gaps but a group of 12 went up the road it wasn't too threatening yet ineos were keen to chase it down they didn't want a group that large going up the road and then when i was watching e3 i saw there was a break of four riders maverick cavani shelling and aaronsmen and between them and the pelton was a break of 40 riders which didn't have any ineos riders in it and a break that big was certainly a problem for ineos so they got to work chasing stringing out the bunch apparently maharich on the descent of the first major climb of the day had gone clear in his patented super tuck position the 40 man group would eventually catch the four escapees and so it formed a larger group with knox camera cataldo verona croix fight writers would regularly try and re-split it all the while luke rowe is like what did i do to deserve the last two days having to keep the break under four minutes for the entire day with just over 50 kilometers to go remy cavania attacked that large breakaway group i think he had james knox still in the wheels back there who could sit on on one of the rollers ineos still pacing they brought the gap down to about three minutes before the last major climb of the day and cavania built up a decent buffer of almost over a minute on that chasing group and because that group was so large often riders would be trying to skip turns they don't have to pull through the same way as if it's a small group and this is that group this isn't the peloton this is the 40 man group chasing remy cavania but after nearly 30 kilometers solo off the front the climb proved to be a bit too much for remy cavania and they ate into his gap very very quickly ineos had the gap at about two minutes two minutes 30 at this point with richard carapaz actually pacing on the climb keeping row and dennis behind he'd be invaluable in the undulating descent into the finish and with the gap to cavania now under 30 seconds and coming near to the crest of the climb bizkara attacked vulcatel using the motorbike photographer as a bit of a draft it's a bit of a false summit this climate is cruel for cavania even when he went over where the kom points were they still had some more climbing to do on a bit of a ridge line before the descent proper and that meant that attacks flew thick and fast verona attacking after leonard cameron's first move off the front stiff and christ spike fiona wisman marking him that trigger had a five second gap on this ridgeline but in the group behind there were teams with multiple riders in that group guerrero and iran for education first nippo kamna tried this for a second time looking iran in the eye but he was marked by dan martin on this short descent section and one of the most threatening moves was verona once again he's looked in very good condition in this catalonia in stage one he was one of the architects of blowing up that race and dropping segan on the climb and still made it over with the lead group for mother star he zoomed away on the extended descent with christ like chasing and leonard cameron just catching up to them we didn't see canon's attack because they brought the motors away from the front of them which is probably a good idea but it was right at this moment from the heli shot just behind stephen christ like he attacks through the middle just as they were getting to the end of this descent and they're still up and down sections which makes it perfect this parkour after the descent for attacks out of this breakaway this was kevna's third move i think that we saw and he was gone quickly building up a 30-second buffer on the chase group the group behind us stopped cooperating particularly well this car attacking going for the minor placings on the stage guerrero for education first the portuguese rider setting after him but leonard campner becoming quite a breakaway specialist i said in the preview we knew from the tour de france last year that he has no problem getting in back-to-back breakaways even on hard stage as we saw in yesterday's stage tried a crazy move very early but ineos shut him down but that meant he lost a lot of time on gc which meant he could get in the breakaway today and take another world tour victory at catalunya for his palmares and camden just seems to have a really good combination of climbing obviously but also being able to attack multiple times from the break descending and maintaining that gap on the flatter or low gradient sections with pretty strong time trialling so a very similar move from him in the twitter france stage 16 when he attacked richard carapaz on one of the last climbs of the day and then tt'ed away to the finish the minor placings ruben guerrero taking second biscara third and dion smith winning his second bunch sprint for minor placings in five days here's the stage results leonard camden first probably should take the most combative rider for the whole of this year's catalonia as well guerrero second biscara third dion smith dan martin maurice knox valtteri christ like despite all the chaos of the day and probably some headaches for ineos there weren't any changes to the gc standings they kept that gap in check adam yates still leading port and grant thomas by 45 and 49 seconds respectively i've been having a lot of questions on the videos with people asking about supertax marriage shown here use it obviously today to great effect uranus rich in stage six and paranese the one rogwich one and we've seen it so much this week in catalonia they've got these wide well-surfaced highway descents that are very long verona camden ineos with castroviejo and dennis chasing camden yesterday a lot of people were saying i thought that was banned why aren't they getting disqualified or sanctioned for that well the rule doesn't apply until april 1 which is in about four days we don't know if it's an april fool's joke either here's the new rule writers must observe the standard position as defined by article 1.308 sitting on the bicycle's top tube is prohibited that article 1.3008 has always existed it's in the general rules of cycling as a sport the rider shall normally assume a sitting position on the bicycle this position requires that the only points of support are the following the feet on the pedals the hands on the handlebars and the seat of the saddle now just a few comments from me putting my lawyer hat on obviously the rules are not very well drafted as normal to start status the new rule says the writers must it's mandatory presumably at all times that they observe the standard position as defined by article 1.3008 which defines the standard position as a sitting position on the bicycle so you're probably saying doesn't that mean sprints are banned if you're sprinting out of the saddle or yes on a literal reading of these new regulations in conjunction with 1.3.08 yes sprints could be prohibited you can also interpret this to say that the supertank isn't necessarily banned if you don't use the top tube as a means of support if you have a gap between your body and the top tube and you still have your hands on the handlebars and your feet on the pedals well technically the super tuck isn't bad i've not really seen any studies showing the frequency and severity of crashes whilst riders are using the supertuck i don't think it exists my view based on watching a lot of cycling is that the existence of bunch sprints is significantly more dangerous in terms of the frequency of crashes and a similar severity of outcome when there are crashes as compared to riders using the super tuck where crashes seem to be very infrequent the next stage being stage six from tarragona to mataro another long stage 193.5 kilometers over 2500 metres climbing and you think remy cavania who's tried hard this week so far to no avail especially coming second again in the tt will be a big contender for this stage he lost all of his time today on that longer climb climbing stage five is not so hard so he's a big shout from a break as well as dion smith for bike exchange either from a break or from a sprint because the sprinter's field is not particularly deep here but i hope you enjoyed this video like it down below if you did let me know what you think about the new supertuck ban or whether you think it's a ban at all i'll see with the highlights at stage six tomorrow
